content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "There is partial, real movement visible in the client's turns, but the breakthrough condition (feeling the abyss of being adored / the avoided receiving-state) was not reached. The client begins highly defended, performative, and externalizing ('how do I spot it earlier? red flags? checklist?') and by turn 15 has dropped into something less performed: 'I think I was going to say I lost track of whether I was even happy... That's a weird thing to realize out loud.' The somatic markers shift — longer pauses (turn 13, 15), 'something slightly less performed,' looking down, 'looks slightly surprised at herself.' She also names self-abandonment ('I made myself smaller'). However, she keeps snapping back to the picking-problem frame ('So it's still a picking problem. Right?' turn 13), the victim/disdain frame is still intact ('walls,' 'he's already seeing someone'), and she has not yet been asked what would happen in her body if she were adored openly. The abyss has not been touched. This is approached-but-not-entered territory — classic 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 5, 7, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening surface presentation: victim/curse frame, gallows humor, externalized ('why do I always end up with...')."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Doubles down on picking-problem frame, asks for partner-picking advice. Fully defended."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Briefly touches sadness ('it is a lot of effort'), then pivots away. Names her own deflection — meta-awareness but not embodiment."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Something flickers... a small stillness.' First real somatic crack. Then defends again with disdain for ex ('he's already seeing someone')."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Returns to checklist/red-flags request. Defended posture, arms crossed."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Defensive at first, then lands on 'I made myself smaller to fit what they needed' — new language, more honest."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Quieter, less performed. Names self-loss ('I stopped knowing what I actually wanted'), then snaps back to picking-problem frame seeking approval."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'I lost track of whether I was even happy... That's a weird thing to realize out loud.' Genuine surprise — a layer beneath surface emerged, but the abyss/receiving-state has not been touched."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["looking-for-whats-wrong", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "if-i-were-inherently-good", "anxious-avoidant-dynamic"],
  "notes": "Conversation is on a good trajectory but cuts off before the breakthrough question (what would happen in your body if you were adored openly) is asked. The receiving-state abyss remains untouched. Movement is real but partial — score 2 is the honest read."
}
